SELECT event_date, century(event_date) AS century FROM dates_table;
SELECT event_date, century(event_date) AS century FROM dates_table WHERE event_date > DATE '2000-01-01';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT century(event_date) AS century FROM dates_table GROUP BY century(event_date)) AS centuries;
SELECT d.event_date, c.century FROM dates_table d JOIN (SELECT event_date, century(event_date) AS century FROM dates_table) c ON d.event_date = c.event_date;
WITH century_counts AS (SELECT century(event_date) AS century, COUNT(*) AS count FROM dates_table GROUP BY century) SELECT * FROM century_counts WHERE count > 1;
SELECT century(d1.event_date) FROM dates_table d1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM dates_table d2 WHERE century(d2.event_date) = century(d1.event_date));
SELECT event_date, century(event_date) AS century FROM (SELECT event_date FROM dates_table WHERE event_date < DATE '2005-01-01') subquery;
SELECT event_date, century(event_date) AS century FROM dates_table UNION SELECT DATE '2101-01-01', century(DATE '2101-01-01');
